title: 1981-04-16_Pat_95.txt
key_claims: Dick questions whether the adventitious psyche (Thomas) actually existed as an independent spirit, or if the "logical operation" (the meta-abstraction) actually *created* the second psyche.

He proposes an inversion of Descartes' *Cogito ergo sum*: "I think, and by thinking I am caused to be." The act of performing the meta-abstraction generates the secondary self in the right hemisphere.

Up until the meta-abstraction occurs, the second psyche (the right hemisphere's consciousness) is merely a biologically latent potential. Once activated, the person ceases to be human and becomes a two-souled God-entity (Ditheon).
This is a massive evolutionary quantum leap: the two-souled human is not human at all, just as a thinking animal is no longer an animal but a man.
recurring_concepts: **The Epiphenomenal World**: The physical world is only an illusion cast to hide the underlying structure of reality. Once Ditheon is achieved, the structure becomes visible.
reading_excerpt: Dick questions whether the adventitious psyche (Thomas) actually existed as an independent spirit, or if the "logical operation" (the meta-abstraction) actually *created* the second psyche.
